description: NFC tableside commerce platform — customer PWA (menu, live bill, pay, auth) + admin PWA (floor view, sessions, feature flags) with multi-vendor payment adapter (Stripe Connect + Square) and modular subroutine architecture

userStories.title: Shared contracts package — core TypeScript types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared TypeScript types for sessions, checks, payers, and menu items so that customer-web and admin-web stay in sync.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/contracts/src/index.ts exports: Session, SessionPayer, CheckSnapshot, CheckLine, MenuItem, MenuCategory, MenuSnapshot, PaymentRecord, ServerRequest, FeatureFlags, RestaurantSettings
CheckSnapshot includes: lines, totalCents, subtotalCents, taxCents, feeCents, amountPaidCents, remainingBalanceCents, version, assignmentSummary
CheckLine includes: id, name, grossCents, assignedCents, assignmentStatus, isTinyCharge, parentId
FeatureFlags is a Record<string, boolean> with known keys typed as optional booleans
packages/contracts/package.json exports main and types fields
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Database schema migrations — sessions and payers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database tables for sessions and payers so the platform can track table sessions and who is paying.
acceptanceCriteria: sessions table: id (uuid), public_token (text unique), table_id (text), restaurant_id (text), status (text default 'active'), created_at (timestamptz)
session_payers table: id (uuid), session_id (uuid FK sessions), display_name (text), contact_info (text), contact_type (text), status (text default 'ordering'), created_at (timestamptz)
Migration file in infra/migrations/ or packages/db/migrations/
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Database schema migrations — payments and requests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database tables for payment records and server requests so we can track payments and assistance.
acceptanceCriteria: payment_records table: id (uuid), session_id (uuid FK), payer_id (uuid FK session_payers), amount_cents (int), tip_cents (int), provider (text), provider_payment_id (text), status (text), created_at (timestamptz)
server_requests table: id (uuid), session_id (uuid FK), request_type (text), notes (text), status (text default 'pending'), created_at (timestamptz)

restaurant_settings table: restaurant_id (text PK), feature_flags (jsonb default '{}'), payment_provider (text default 'stripe'), stripe_connect_account_id (text), square_merchant_id (text), updated_at (timestamptz)
Migration file created
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Payment adapter interface + Stripe Connect implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a PaymentAdapter interface with a Stripe Connect implementation so payments can be processed through a vendor-agnostic layer.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/payment-adapters/src/interface.ts exports PaymentAdapter interface with: createPaymentIntent, confirmPayment, refund, getStatus
packages/payment-adapters/src/stripe-adapter.ts implements PaymentAdapter using stripe SDK
createPaymentIntent accepts: amountCents, tipCents, restaurantStripeAccountId, idempotencyKey, metadata
transfer_data.destination set to restaurantStripeAccountId on payment intent
application_fee_amount calculated from platform fee percentage (env: PLATFORM_FEE_PERCENT, default 2)
packages/payment-adapters/package.json exports correctly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Square payment adapter implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Square adapter implementing PaymentAdapter so restaurants using Square can process payments without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/payment-adapters/src/square-adapter.ts implements PaymentAdapter using squareup SDK
createPaymentIntent maps to Square CreatePayment API
Active adapter selected by PAYMENT_PROVIDER env var: 'stripe' uses StripeAdapter, 'square' uses SquareAdapter
packages/payment-adapters/src/index.ts exports getPaymentAdapter(provider) factory function
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Feature flags API route + useFeatureFlags hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a feature flags API and React hook so each feature module can conditionally render based on restaurant settings.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/restaurant/[restaurantId]/flags returns FeatureFlags JSON from restaurant_settings
apps/customer-web/src/lib/feature-flags.ts exports getFeatureFlags(restaurantId) server function
apps/customer-web/src/hooks/useFeatureFlags.ts exports useFeatureFlags() client hook (fetches on mount, 30s refresh)
Known flag keys: menu_ordering, kitchen_submit, live_bill, pay_tab, request_server, loyalty, phone_otp, email_auth, social_auth, guest_checkout, apple_pay, google_pay, samsung_pay
Default: all flags true if no restaurant_settings row exists
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: JWT auth utility + middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I need JWT signing/verification utilities and a Next.js middleware so authenticated routes are protected.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/customer-web/src/lib/auth.ts exports: signJWT(payload), verifyJWT(token), getSessionUser(request)
JWT payload: { payerId, sessionId, contactType, contact }
JWT stored in httpOnly cookie named 'taps_session', 24hr expiry
apps/customer-web/src/middleware.ts protects /api/pay/* routes — returns 401 if no valid JWT
JWT_SECRET read from env
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Phone OTP auth API routes (send + verify)
userStories.description: As a diner, I want to receive an OTP on my phone and verify it to authenticate before paying.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/send-otp accepts { phone: string, sessionId: string } — sends OTP via Twilio Verify
POST /api/auth/verify-otp accepts { phone: string, code: string, sessionId: string } — verifies OTP
On success: upserts session_payers row with contact_info=phone, contact_type='phone', sets JWT cookie, returns { payerId, displayName }
On failure: returns 400 with { error: 'Invalid code' }
TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, TWILIO_VERIFY_SERVICE_SID read from env
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Email auth API route
userStories.description: As a diner, I want to sign in with email and password so I can pay and receive a receipt by email.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/email accepts { email: string, password: string, sessionId: string }
Looks up user by email in a users table (email, password_hash, display_name)
On success: upserts session_payers row with contact_type='email', sets JWT cookie, returns { payerId, displayName }
POST /api/auth/register accepts { email, password, displayName, sessionId } — creates user + payer
Passwords hashed with bcrypt (10 rounds)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Session summary API route
userStories.description: As the customer PWA, I need to resolve a publicToken to session context so the app knows which table and restaurant it is serving.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/session/[token]/summary returns: { session: { id, tableId, restaurantId, status }, access: { publicAccessAllowed }, payers: SessionPayer[], settlement: { tableComplete, payerCompletionCount, totalPayerCount } }
publicAccessAllowed is true if session status is 'active'
404 if token not found
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Menu API route
userStories.description: As the customer PWA, I need to fetch the restaurant menu so diners can browse items.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/menu/[restaurantId] returns MenuSnapshot: { categories: MenuCategory[], items: MenuItem[] }
MenuItem includes: id, name, description, basePriceCents, categoryId, availability ('available'|'sold_out'|'hidden'), modifiers
Seed data included for Black+Blue Toronto: Steaks & Mains, Starters, Cocktails, Desserts categories with 4-6 items each
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Check/bill API route
userStories.description: As the customer PWA, I need to fetch the live check snapshot so diners can see the current bill.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/session/[token]/check returns CheckSnapshot with lines, subtotalCents, taxCents, feeCents, totalCents, amountPaidCents, remainingBalanceCents, version, assignmentSummary
assignmentSummary includes: unassignedLineItemIds[], unassignedTinyItemIds[]
Lines include parentId for modifier hierarchy
Seed check data for demo tokens (pub_demo_table_10, pub_demo_table_12)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Order submission API route
userStories.description: As a diner, I need to submit my cart to the kitchen via the API.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/session/[token]/order accepts { lines: { menuItemId, quantity, modifiers, notes }[] }
Creates order_lines records in DB linked to session
Returns { orderId, status: 'submitted', lines: [...] }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Payment intent creation API route
userStories.description: As the customer PWA, I need to create a payment intent server-side before charging the diner.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/pay/[token]/intent accepts { amountCents, tipCents, splitMethod: 'evenly'|'items'|'custom', splitN?: number } — requires valid JWT
Looks up restaurant payment_provider and credentials from restaurant_settings
Calls getPaymentAdapter(provider).createPaymentIntent(...) with idempotency key = sessionId+payerId+amountCents
Returns { clientSecret, provider, intentId }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Payment webhook handler + payer status update
userStories.description: As the system, I need to handle Stripe/Square webhooks so payer status updates when payment succeeds.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/webhooks/stripe validates Stripe-Signature header against STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
On payment_intent.succeeded: updates payment_records status to 'completed', updates session_payers status to 'completed'
Recalculates remainingBalanceCents on check snapshot
Triggers receipt delivery (calls sendReceipt service)
Returns 200 immediately, processing async
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Receipt delivery service (SMS + email)
userStories.description: As a diner, I want to receive an itemized receipt after paying via SMS or email.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/customer-web/src/lib/receipt.ts exports sendReceipt({ payerId, paymentRecordId })
Loads payer contact_type and contact_info from DB
If contact_type='phone': sends SMS via Twilio with itemized receipt text (restaurant name, table, items, subtotal, tip, total)
If contact_type='email': sends HTML email via Resend with same data
RESEND_API_KEY and RECEIPT_FROM_EMAIL read from env
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server request API route
userStories.description: As a diner, I need to submit a server assistance request via the API.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/session/[token]/request accepts { requestType: 'water'|'utensils'|'ice'|'refill'|'other', notes?: string }
Creates server_requests record with status='pending'
Returns { requestId, status: 'pending' }
GET /api/session/[token]/request returns current server availability and pending request count
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Admin API — table states + floor view data
userStories.description: As the admin PWA, I need an API that returns all active table states for the floor view.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/admin/floor returns: { tables: TableState[], stats: { activeTables, totalOutstandingCents, alertCount } }
TableState includes: tableId, sessionId, status ('available'|'just_seated'|'eating'|'about_to_pay'|'assistance_requested'), guestCount, timeActiveMs, remainingBalanceCents
Status derived from session + check + server_requests state
assistance_requested if any pending server_request exists for session
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Admin API — sessions list + exceptions
userStories.description: As the admin PWA, I need APIs for the sessions list and exceptions.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/admin/sessions returns paginated list of sessions with: tableId, sessionId, checkVersion, status, remainingBalanceCents, payersDone, payersTotal, publicAccessAllowed, exceptionCount
GET /api/admin/exceptions returns sessions with unassigned lines or close-validation failures
POST /api/admin/exceptions/[id]/resolve marks exception resolved
POST /api/admin/sessions/[id]/mark-served updates session status
POST /api/admin/sessions/[id]/clear sets session status to 'closed'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Admin API — feature flags CRUD
userStories.description: As a restaurant manager, I need to update feature flags via the API so the admin dashboard can toggle features.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/admin/settings/[restaurantId]/flags returns current FeatureFlags
PATCH /api/admin/settings/[restaurantId]/flags accepts partial FeatureFlags, merges into restaurant_settings.feature_flags, updates updated_at
Response includes updated flags + last_changed_at
